specifications.name: Project
specifications.description:
All data sets shall consist of a single IfcProject instance, which identifies the overall project, provides defaults for units, and holds a graph of references to all data within scope.

Can't see any possibilities to have unit requirements in IDS.
The RAR spoecifies:
- Length Unit: Default unit for length. For imperial, inches are recommended. SI Meters are thedefault if not provided.
- Angle Unit: Default unit for angles. Degrees are recommended, and are the default if not provided.
- Mass Unit: Default unit for mass. For imperial, pounds are recommended. SI Kilograms are the default if not provided.
Temperature Unit: Default unit for temperature. For imperial, fahrenheit is recommended. SI Celcius is the default if not provided.
applicability: All IFCPROJECT data

specifications.name: Site
specifications.description:
Data sets shall include at least one site. The site may define existing and proposed terrain within vicinity of the bridge project and contain alignment(s), bridge(s) and potentially other structures such as the road/rail supported by the bridge. Boring points may be included as objects contained within the site.

Comment: The RAR specifies a mandatory Site. However here it is made optional. Also the reference elevation is required in the RAR. However, the reference elevation should be present in the CRS reference.

specifications.name: Bridge
specifications.description: Each bridge structure is captured within a definition describing the span location of a bridge relative to an alignment. The extents of the bridge are defined using an Axis curve, where IfcOffsetCurveByDistances would fit most scenarios – referencing an IfcAlignmentCurve with starting and ending distances along the curve, and typically no laterial or vertical offsets.

specifications.name: Alignment
specifications.description:
Horizontal and vertical alignment curves provide the underlying placement for all
components in a bridge plan.

Missing: Axis, Placement (not possible in IDS)
applicability: All IFCALIGNMENT data

requirements.label: IFCRELAGGREGATES
requirements.description: An element must have an IFCRELAGGREGATES relationship with an IFCPROJECT

specifications.name: Substructure
specifications.description: The substructure of a bridge refers to elements that transfer loads into the ground.
applicability: All IFCBRIDGEPART data of type SUBSTRUCTURE

requirements.label: IFCRELAGGREGATES
requirements.description: An element must have an IFCRELAGGREGATES relationship with an IFCBRIDGE

specifications.name: Superstructure
specifications.description:
The superstructure of a bridge refers to those elements than span horizontally to carry loads onto substructures.

Missing: Alignments
applicability: All IFCBRIDGEPART data of type SUPERSTRUCTURE

requirements.label: IFCRELAGGREGATES
requirements.description: An element must have an IFCRELAGGREGATES relationship with an IFCBRIDGE

specifications.name: Girders
applicability: All IFCELEMENTASSEMBLY data of type GIRDER

requirements.label: IFCRELCONTAINEDINSPATIALSTRUCTURE
requirements.description: An element must have an IFCRELCONTAINEDINSPATIALSTRUCTURE relationship with an IFCBRIDGEPART of predefined type SUPERSTRUCTURE

specifications.name: Deck
specifications.description: A bridge deck is comprised of those elements used for conveying traffic but do not perform structural functions of the superstructure.
applicability: All IFCBRIDGEPART data of type DECK

requirements.label: IFCRELAGGREGATES
requirements.description: An element must have an IFCRELAGGREGATES relationship with an IFCBRIDGE

specifications.name: Deck segments
applicability: All IFCBRIDGEPART data of type DECK_SEGMENT

requirements.label: IFCRELAGGREGATES
requirements.description: An element must have an IFCRELAGGREGATES relationship with an IFCBRIDGEPART of predefined type DECK

specifications.name: Abutments
specifications.description: Abutments refer to substructures at the ends of a bridge. They may be composed of wing walls (on each side), head wall, stem wall, and cone.
applicability: All IFCELEMENTASSEMBLY data of type ABUTMENT
An element with an IFCRELCONTAINEDINSPATIALSTRUCTURE relationship with an IFCBRIDGEPART

specifications.name: Piers
specifications.description:
Piers are decomposed into elements according to connectivity, indicating construction joints. Reinforcing may be indicated within inner elements such as footings, columns, members, and walls (see documentation at corresponding elements for usage); such reinforcing shouldreflect how it is to be placed at time of construction such that rebar connecting between elements is projected out of the element where it is initially placed.
Abutments and piers are placed relative to the horizontal alignment curve (NOT the vertical alignment curve), with components placed according to Cartesian placement within. This reflects positioning as typically indicated on construction plans, where all dimension lines are based on Cartesian positioning relative to the position and orientation of the station along the horizontal alignment curve.

Missing: Alignment, Location (not possible with IDS)
applicability: All IFCELEMENTASSEMBLY data of type PIER

requirements.label: IFCRELCONTAINEDINSPATIALSTRUCTURE
requirements.description: An element must have an IFCRELCONTAINEDINSPATIALSTRUCTURE relationship with an IFCBRIDGEPART of predefined type SUBSTRUCTURE

specifications.name: Piles
applicability: All IFCPILE data

specifications.name: Surface Proposed
specifications.description: The ground surface of the site indicating final conditions.

specifications.name: Surface Existing

specifications.name: Boring Points (IfcGeographicElement)
specifications.description:
Boring points indicating soil layers and properties at discrete locations.
Site grading is indicated using several geometric structures, for which contour lines or the elevation at any point may be derived. The IfcGeographicElement entity with
PredefinedType set to SOILBORING (new) may be used to indicate soil borings at particular points.

Missing: Location (not possible in IDS)
applicability: All IFCGEOGRAPHICELEMENT data of type SOIL_BORING_POINT

requirements.label: IFCRELCONTAINEDINSPATIALSTRUCTURE
requirements.description: An element must have an IFCRELCONTAINEDINSPATIALSTRUCTURE relationship with an {'enumeration': ['IFCSITE', 'IFCBRIDGE']}

specifications.name: Material
specifications.description: Materials are defined on elements to be constructed (e.g. concrete), fabricated (e.g. steel girders), and that exist on site (e.g. soil borings), indicating material category, classification, and structural properties.
applicability: All IFCMATERIAL data

metadata.@instructions: Material classification according to the respective authority (e.g. ASTM)
Category of material, where if provided must be one of "Steel", "Concrete", "Wood", "Plastic", "Glass", "Earth".
Material mass density.

requirements.label: Pset_MaterialCommon.MassDensity
requirements.description: MassDensity data may be provided in the dataset Pset_MaterialCommon

metadata.@instructions: A measure of the Young's modulus of elasticity of the material.

requirements.label: Pset_MaterialMechanical.YoungModulus
requirements.description: YoungModulus data may be provided in the dataset Pset_MaterialMechanical

metadata.@instructions: A measure of the shear modulus of elasticity of the material.

requirements.label: Pset_MaterialMechanical.ShearModulus
requirements.description: ShearModulus data may be provided in the dataset Pset_MaterialMechanical

metadata.@instructions: A measure of the expansion coefficient for warming up the material about one Kelvin.

requirements.label: Pset_MaterialMechanical.ThermalExpansionCoefficient
requirements.description: ThermalExpansionCoefficient data may be provided in the dataset Pset_MaterialMechanical

metadata.@instructions: [If Category=”Concrete”] The compressive strength of the concrete.

requirements.label: Pset_MaterialConcrete.CompressiveStrength
requirements.description: CompressiveStrength data may be provided in the dataset Pset_MaterialConcrete

metadata.@instructions: [If Category=”Steel”] A measure of the yield stress (or characteristic 0.2 percent proof stress) of the material.

requirements.label: Pset_MaterialSteel.YieldStress
requirements.description: YieldStress data may be provided in the dataset Pset_MaterialSteel

specifications.description: Each pier may have one or more stems, separated laterally.

specifications.description: Each pier may have a top that spans stem(s). If such cap is above a bearing, then it is modelled as part of the superstructure.

specifications.name: Apron
specifications.description: A bridge apron is a device to protect a river bank or river bed against scour; a shield (source: http://sdrc.lib.uiowa.edu/eng/bridges/WaddellGlossary/GlossA.htm).
applicability: All IFCBUILDINGELEMENTPART data of type APRON

requirements.label: IFCRELCONTAINEDINSPATIALSTRUCTURE
requirements.description: An element must have an IFCRELCONTAINEDINSPATIALSTRUCTURE relationship with an IFCBRIDGEPART of predefined type SUBSTRUCTURE

specifications.name: Arch
specifications.description: An arch refers to a hyperbolic member that supports vertical loads at intervals along its span.
applicability: All IFCELEMENTASSEMBLY data of type ARCH

requirements.label: IFCRELCONTAINEDINSPATIALSTRUCTURE
requirements.description: An element must have an IFCRELCONTAINEDINSPATIALSTRUCTURE relationship with an IFCBRIDGEPART of predefined type SUBSTRUCTURE

specifications.name: Footing
specifications.description:
Footings are typically described geometrically by enclosed polygonal areas extruded
vertically according to footing depth. For stepped footings, multiple extruded solids may be used, however they must not intersect.

Missing: Geometry, Connection to Piles, Reinforcing

Piles supporting footings are linked according to a connection relationship.
Reinforcing is typically modelled as aggregation of Footing - Footing/Reinforcement
applicability: All IFCFOOTING data of type PAD_FOOTING

requirements.label: IFCRELCONTAINEDINSPATIALSTRUCTURE
requirements.description: An element must have an IFCRELCONTAINEDINSPATIALSTRUCTURE relationship with an IFCBRIDGEPART of predefined type {'enumeration': ['SUBSTRUCTURE', 'FOUNDATION']}

metadata.@instructions: Name of the footing for referencing purposes, as would be found on construction plans.
Material properties of the footing, indicating concrete strength.

specifications.name: Pile
specifications.description:
Piles are typically described geometrically by a circular profile extruded vertically according to pile depth. For multiple piles, mapped representation may be used to efficiently place piles of similar dimensions in multiple locations.

Missing: Cross section, Batter

Piles supporting footings are linked according to a connection relationship.
applicability: All IFCPILE data

requirements.label: IFCRELCONTAINEDINSPATIALSTRUCTURE
requirements.description: An element must have an IFCRELCONTAINEDINSPATIALSTRUCTURE relationship with an IFCBRIDGEPART of predefined type SUBSTRUCTURE

metadata.@instructions: Name of pile arrangement for referencing purposes, as found on construction plans.
Material of piles.

specifications.name: Hat Stone
specifications.description: Hat Stone refers to a top course on an abutment or culvert.
applicability: All IFCBEAM data of type HATSTONE

specifications.name: Girder
specifications.description:
Bridge girders refer to horizontal support beams that span along the alignment of a bridge.

For steel girders, this refers to each girder line, decomposed into beam segments.
For concrete box girders, this refers to the overall box girder, typically connected to the bridge deck via a keyed construction joint with adjoining reinforcing.

Missing: Alignment, Axis, IfcRelDefinesByType
applicability: All IFCELEMENTASSEMBLY data of type GIRDER

requirements.label: IFCRELCONTAINEDINSPATIALSTRUCTURE
requirements.description: An element must have an IFCRELCONTAINEDINSPATIALSTRUCTURE relationship with an IFCBRIDGEPART of predefined type SUPERSTRUCTURE

metadata.@instructions: Name of the girder line for referencing purposes as would be found on construction plans.

specifications.name: Boring Points 2 (IfcBorehole)
specifications.description:
Boring points indicating soil layers and properties at discrete locations.
Site grading is indicated using several geometric structures, for which contour lines or the elevation at any point may be derived. The IfcGeographicElement entity with
PredefinedType set to SOILBORING (new) may be used to indicate soil borings at particular points.
applicability: All IFCBOREHOLE data

requirements.label: IFCRELCONTAINEDINSPATIALSTRUCTURE
requirements.description: An element must have an IFCRELCONTAINEDINSPATIALSTRUCTURE relationship with an {'enumeration': ['IFCSITE', 'IFCBRIDGE']}

specifications.name: Girder Type
specifications.description: Template defining general construction that may be used across projects.
applicability: All IFCELEMENTASSEMBLYTYPE data of type GIRDER

specifications.name: Girder Segment
specifications.description:
Girder segments refer to discrete sections along a girder line. They may be modelled as one object that encapsulates the overall cross section (e.g. I-Shape steel, arbitrary precast profile), or as separate objects (e.g. steel flange plates, steel web plate). Each segment may have a constant cross section, a tapered cross section (linearly interpolated from start to end), a variable cross section (linearly interpolated at multiple points between start and end), or free-form geometry.

Missing: Solid Geometry, Connection*, Reinforcing. Tendons, Camber, Stiffeners, Shear Studs
applicability: All IFCBEAM data of type GIRDER_SEGMENT

requirements.label: IFCRELAGGREGATES
requirements.description: An element must have an IFCRELAGGREGATES relationship with an {'enumeration': ['IFCELEMENTASSEMBLY', 'IFCELEMENTASSEMBLYTYPE']} of predefined type GIRDER

metadata.@instructions: Name of the girder segment for referencing purposes as would be found on construction plans.

specifications.name: Plates
specifications.description:
Built-up girders may be decomposed into plates (or members) for web, flanges, cover plates, longitudinal stiffeners, and vertical stiffeners.

Comment: Not adding any partOf requirements since plates and members can occur in different assamblies (e.g. also railings).
applicability: All IFCPLATE data

specifications.name: Cross Frame
specifications.description:
Bridge cross frames connect two girders laterally.
Cross-framing between girders may be described using templates of member configurations. Such cross framing is captured within components, using standard shapes (e.g. AISC in U.S.) where applicable. For curved alignments where girders are placed at different elevations, members must be placed relative to the girders at each side, for which positioning is defined relative to alignment curves.

Missing: Position (not possible in IDS), Girder Segment Left/Right (not possible in IDS
applicability: All IFCELEMENTASSEMBLY data of type CROSS_BRACING

metadata.@instructions: Name of cross frame occurrence as would be identified on construction plans.

specifications.name: Diaphragm
specifications.description:
Diaphragms refer to sections of bridge girders immediately above supporting structures that provide additional lateral and vertical support.
Diaphragms are modeled similarly as concrete girder segments, except are distinguished according to a predefined type (e.g. IfcBeamTypeEnum.DIAPHRAGM), and have connections to underlying supports.

Missing: Solid geometry, Connection*, Reinforcing, Tendons
applicability: All IFCBEAM data of type DIAPHRAGM

requirements.label: IFCRELCONTAINEDINSPATIALSTRUCTURE
requirements.description: An element must have an IFCRELCONTAINEDINSPATIALSTRUCTURE relationship with an IFCBRIDGEPART of predefined type SUPERSTRUCTURE

metadata.@instructions: Name of the diaphragm for referencing purposes as would be found on construction plans.

specifications.name: Truss
specifications.description:
Trusses refer to a framework of linear structural elements in tension and compression for supporting the span of a bridge superstructure. Within a truss, struts refer to compression elements, and ties refer to tension elements.

Missing: Connections. Also specified IfcPlate generically (without GUSSET_PLATE PDT) as part of a truss.
applicability: All IFCELEMENTASSEMBLY data of type TRUSS

specifications.name: Deck Span
specifications.description:
Bridge deck spans represent the surface of a bridge. They may be decomposed into
segments and components.
applicability: All IFCELEMENTASSEMBLY data of type DECK

specifications.name: Deck Segment
specifications.description:
This entity may be used to model segments of a bridge deck, separated by construction orexpansion joint. Geometry for bridge decks is typically represented using IfcSectionedSolidHorizontal for defining a cross section that may potentially vary along an alignment.

Comment: Geometric requirements, connection reqirements, reinforcing and drainage.
applicability: All IFCBRIDGEPART data of type DECK_SEGMENT

requirements.label: IFCRELAGGREGATES
requirements.description: An element must have an IFCRELAGGREGATES relationship with an IFCBRIDGEPART of predefined type DECK

metadata.@instructions: Name of the deck segment for referencing purposes as would be found on construction plans.
Material of the deck segment.

specifications.name: Parapet
specifications.description:
This entity may be used to model barriers of constant cross-section, or architectural railings.

Barriers may be defined as a constant cross-section placed along the alignment at either edge of a bridge deck or anywhere in between.

Comment: Geometry, Connections, Reinforcing, Conduit
applicability: All IFCWALL data of type PARAPET

requirements.label: IFCRELCONTAINEDINSPATIALSTRUCTURE
requirements.description: An element must have an IFCRELCONTAINEDINSPATIALSTRUCTURE relationship with an IFCBRIDGEPART of predefined type {'enumeration': ['DECK', 'DECK_SEGMENT']}

metadata.@instructions: Name of the parapet segment for referencing purposes as would be found on construction plans.
Material of the parapet.

specifications.name: Railing
specifications.description:
This entity may be used to model barriers of constant cross-section, or architectural railings.

Barriers may be defined as a constant cross-section placed along the alignment at either edge of a bridge deck or anywhere in between.

Comment: Geometry, Connections, Reinforcing, Conduit
applicability: All IFCRAILING data of type GUARDRAIL

requirements.label: IFCRELCONTAINEDINSPATIALSTRUCTURE

metadata.@instructions: Name of the railing segment for referencing purposes as would be found on construction plans.

specifications.name: Approach slab
specifications.description: An approach slab refers to a slab providing transition between a bridge and road pavement, where an expansion joint allows for differential settlement, temperature changes, and freeze/thaw effects.
applicability: All IFCSLAB data of type APPROACH_SLAB

requirements.label: IFCRELCONTAINEDINSPATIALSTRUCTURE
requirements.description: An element must have an IFCRELCONTAINEDINSPATIALSTRUCTURE relationship with an IFCBRIDGEPART of predefined type {'enumeration': ['DECK', 'DECK_SEGMENT']}

specifications.name: Cornice
specifications.description: A cornice is construction eveloping the sides of a bridge protecting the deck.
applicability: All IFCBEAM data of type CORNICE

requirements.label: IFCRELCONTAINEDINSPATIALSTRUCTURE
requirements.description: An element must have an IFCRELCONTAINEDINSPATIALSTRUCTURE relationship with an IFCBRIDGEPART of predefined type {'enumeration': ['DECK', 'DECK_SEGMENT']}

specifications.name: Waterproofing
specifications.description: Waterproofing membranes are applied on top of deck segments.
applicability: All IFCCOVERING data of type MEMBRANE

requirements.label: IFCRELCONTAINEDINSPATIALSTRUCTURE
requirements.description: An element must have an IFCRELCONTAINEDINSPATIALSTRUCTURE relationship with an IFCBRIDGEPART of predefined type {'enumeration': ['DECK', 'DECK_SEGMENT']}

specifications.name: Bearing
specifications.description:
Bearings refer to elements connecting substructure elements to superstructure elements where movement is allowed along one or more degrees of freedom.

Comment: Type relationship, Mechanical constraint, Connections
applicability: All IFCBEARING data

metadata.@instructions: Name of bearing for referencing purposes as would be found on construction plans.

specifications.name: Bearing type
specifications.description:
Type of bearing, where common geometry and properties may be defined.

Comment: Occurrence relationship, Mechanical constraint
applicability: All IFCBEARINGTYPE data

specifications.name: Joint
specifications.description:
An expansion joint is an assembly connection between construction elements to allow for thermal differential expansions.

Comment: Type relationship, Expansion extent, Connections.
applicability: All IFCDISCRETEACCESSORY data of type EXPANSION_JOINT_DEVICE

metadata.@instructions: Name of joint for referencing purposes as would be found on construction plans.

specifications.name: Joint type
specifications.description:
Type of joint, where common geometry and properties may be defined.

Comment: Occurrence relationship, Expansion extent, Connections.
applicability: All IFCDISCRETEACCESSORYTYPE data of type EXPANSION_JOINT_DEVICE

specifications.name: Members
specifications.description:
Built-up girders may be decomposed into plates (or members) for web, flanges, cover plates, longitudinal stiffeners, and vertical stiffeners.

Comment: Not adding any partOf requirements since plates and members can occur in different assamblies (e.g. also railings).
applicability: All {'enumeration': ['IFCMEMBER', 'IFCMEMBERTYPE']} data
